cards.definition: The ______ is the condition of the atmosphere — whether it is hot, cold, sunny, or rainy.
cards.definitionExample: The weather in summer is warm and sunny.
examples: The weather forecast says it will rain tomorrow.

cards.definition: To ______ means to go from one place to another, usually over a long distance.
cards.definitionExample: I love to travel to new countries.
examples: She wants to travel around Europe next summer.
It takes two hours to travel from here to the capital.

cards.definition: To ______ means to choose between two or more possibilities.
cards.definitionExample: I need to decide what to study at university.
examples: Have you decided where to go on holiday?
It was difficult to decide between the two jobs.

cards.definition: If you are ______, you have a lot of things to do and not much free time.
cards.definitionExample: She is very busy at work this week.
examples: I can't meet you today — I'm too busy.
The city centre is always busy on Saturdays.

cards.definition: To ______ something means to keep it in your memory or bring it back to your mind.
cards.definitionExample: Do you remember our first English lesson?
examples: I can't remember where I put my keys.

cards.definition: To ______ means to make something clear so that someone understands it.
cards.definitionExample: Can you explain this grammar rule to me?
examples: The teacher explained the new topic clearly.
He could not explain why he was late.

cards.definition: A ______ is a piece of information that you send to or leave for someone.
cards.definitionExample: She sent me a message on my phone.
examples: I received a message from my friend this morning.
Can you leave a message for him, please?
